name: French Toast- "Best Egg Bread i Ever Had
recipeIngredient: Orange peel in twisted spirals for garnish
OR Orange slice wedges for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the milk in a saucepan; add the sugar, stirring to dissolve.
Remove from heat and cool slightly.
2. Beat eggs slightly and mix with vanilla, grated orange peel (dried is fine), ground cinnamon, and salt. Add to milk and sugar mixture and stir well.
3. Dip each slice of bread into the egg mixture until coated. Place slices in a single layer in a shallow dish; pour any remaining mixture over the slices. Cover with wax paper or place covered pan in refrigerator for 30 minutes.
4. Heat canola oil in a skillet to medium-high heat. Fry bread slices until golden brown. Warm maple syrup under hot water or in microwave for 15 seconds.
5. Serve 2 slices per person, sprinkling confectioners' sugar and ground cinnamon over each (or sprinkle with cinnamon-sugar). Garnish with orange peel twisted into spirals or orange slice wedges. Pass warm maple syrup at the table.
Note: This recipe was inspired by several sources and my own ideas. It was considered the "best egg bread I ever had" by my husband. Edited for MasterCook by K. Hudson Lipin, 07/05/99.
